name: Monte de les Agulles y la Morella por la Cueva de Cassimanya — circular desde Begues
region_intro: Hiking around Begues offers diverse landscapes within the Garraf Massif, characterized by its distinctive karst topography. The region features weathered limestone formations, valleys, and significant peaks such as La Morella and Montau. Trails often provide panoramic views across the Garraf Natural Park and towards the coast. The terrain includes a mix of rugged paths and more gentle routes, suitable for various hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Begues The most popular hiking route is Monte de les Agulles y la Morella por la Cueva de Cassimanya — circular desde Begues, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 17 minutes to complete, offering views of La Morella. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Fountains (PR-C 160), a moderate 8.6 miles (13.9 km) path. This route connects various springs, providing a pleasant walk through the natural surroundings. Local hikers also love the Castillo de l'Eramprunya, roca del Barret y Cueva de les Agulles — circular desde Begues, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Begues is defined by its karst landscape, significant peaks like La Morella and Montau, and historical dry stone constructions. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more demanding hikes. The routes in Begues are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 330 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore Begues's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Begues?
A: Begues offers a wide selection of hiking trails, with over 50 routes available for exploration within the Garraf Massif and surrounding areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Begues?
A: The terrain around Begues is diverse, characterized by the distinctive karst landscape of the Garraf Natural Park. You'll find weathered limestone formations, valleys, and significant peaks like La Morella and Montau. Trails range from rugged paths to more gentle routes, offering a mix of challenging ascents and pleasant walks.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Begues?
A: Yes, Begues offers several easier paths suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, the region has trails for various fitness levels, including those lasting less than two hours. Look for routes described as 'easy' in the guide for suitable options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Begues?
A: The Garraf Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife. Always check local signage for specific regulations on individual trails.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes starting from Begues?
A: Yes, many routes in Begues are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357635559/monte-de-les-agulles-y-la-morella-por-la-cueva-de-cassimanya-circular-desde-begues" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de les Agulles y la Morella por la Cueva de Cassimanya — circular desde Begues&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail offering views of La Morella.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover while hiking?
A: Begues is rich in natural landmarks. You can enjoy panoramic views from peaks like La Morella, which on clear days can stretch to Mallorca. Other notable viewpoints include El Mur (the 'viewpoint of silence'), offering tranquil views of the Collserola mountain range and the Llobregat Delta. The area also features unique geological formations like the Pi Gros, the remains of a historic sunyer pine.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Begues?
A: Many hiking excursions in Begues depart directly from the village center, where parking facilities are typically available. For trails deeper within the Garraf Natural Park, designated parking areas are usually found near trailheads. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended parking spots.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Begues?
A: Begues is accessible by public transport, which can bring you to the village center. From there, many trails are directly accessible. For more remote trailheads within the Garraf Natural Park, public transport options might be limited, so planning ahead is recommended.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Begues?
A: The best time to hike in Begues is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially in the exposed karst areas, while winter offers crisp, clear days with excellent visibility, though some paths might be cooler.

Q: Are there any historical sites or unique geological features to see?
A: Absolutely. The region features historical dry stone constructions and old lime kilns scattered throughout the municipality. Geologically, the Garraf Natural Park is famous for its karst formations, including caves and dolines. The Can Sadurní cave is also an important archaeological site with prehistoric remains.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Begues?
A: The trails in Begues are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 330 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning panoramic views from peaks like La Morella, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Begues offers challenging hikes for those seeking more demanding routes. The Garraf Massif includes significant peaks like Montau (658 meters), the highest in the massif, providing opportunities for strenuous climbs and elevated perspectives. There are 8 difficult routes listed in the area, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357226129/plana-novella-and-les-burigues-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plana Novella and Les Burigues Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 20 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I explore caves or chasms in the Garraf Natural Park near Begues?
A: Yes, the Garraf Natural Park is known for its underground geological features. The area boasts the deepest cave in Garraf, and routes like the 'Ferret, or Karst route,' allow visitors to explore cavities and rock formations. Notable caves include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Riera Caves&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3843406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de Cal Muntaner&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: To the north of the Serra del Clos and close to the Puig de les Agulletes, this cave appears which is located on the ledge of the mountain itself, in an area of cliffs and you have to watch carefully where you put your feet when walking.
Sierra del Parque Natural del Garraf

La Morella is the second highest peak in the Garraf Massif after Montau (658 m), at 593.6 meters. It is located in the municipality of Begues, in the Baix Llobregat region. Its surroundings contain several chasms. There is also the tail of the old Garraf landfill, currently being restored. At the summit, we can find a geodesic vertex. The peak is included in the FEEC's list of the 100 peaks.

A long-distance trail (GR 92 Mediterranean Trail) passes through this mountain peak, on its 21st stage from Bruguers to Garraf. It meets the local short-distance trail (SL-C 99), which is 6.1 km long, running from the Cal Ganxo Environmental Activities Center (Castelldefels) to La Morella. If we follow the GR 92 towards the town of Garraf, 3.5 km away we reach the La Pleta Information Centre in Garraf Park.
